name: Wrack
rules_text: The ruin of magical ambition can come in many forms. This is a strangely pure and blinding sweetness, sharp and transcendent. Its tragedy is its swift passing.

name: Forced March
rules_text: "The mettle of a leader is tested in her ability to deploy her troops. The army of a great general will walk into the fire for her." -- Ngozi, The Book of War

name: Rite of Growth
rules_text: The chosen warrior wanders the Cessao to find the World Snake, who wraps around him and breaks all the bones in his body. He heals as a giant.

name: Jinx
rules_text: "In some cases the Misfit Transformation discards the body entirely. The new being spends its extremely long life looking for a friend." -- Al-Talataa Umunayyil, Tan'u'zhadhi Academy

name: Veteran
rules_text: "Young Vela took to the sword as one possessed. I'll swear I saw the General's spirit in his face." -- Kyman Firth, "The Siege of Groven's Weald"

name: Deluge
rules_text:
Shinies floated
bluemen walked
Beards and shades
on mountains stalked
In the endless rushing wave
Heroes met a sodden grave
-- Human children's rhyme

name: Barkskin
rules_text: Denizens of nature commonly develop hardened bark skin as they grow older, some finally losing themselves to become one with the forest.

name: Beast's Embrace
rules_text: "When the Mage put the Beast in him, he screamed. Afterwards, he was the Mage's pet, and nothing could hurt him. But the agony he had in his eyes " -- Jan Palczred, mercenary

name: Altar of Blood
rules_text: "'You have freed these villagers' bodies', Zana cried, 'but their souls are slaves to Abomination yet! Come -- I will show you where the Altar is hid.'" -- Journal of Lienna: Volume VII

name: Healing Hands
rules_text: "Then graceful Lady Adriel knelt to lay her golden hands upon me, and at once my agony was replaced by sweet peace." -- Theriel of Caenedor

name: Insurrection
rules_text: "When the people learned what Odar had done, they drove him from the city. But the madness stayed, and they strove with one another until Ogi returned." -- The Ballad of Odar

name: Legion of the Dead
rules_text: Nomadic.

name: Fortify
rules_text: Walls, vaulted chambers, arches, buttresses and gates all lead to a competent defense.

name: Change of Heart
rules_text: "The town seemed to move of a body. Without word or signal, they put down their ploughs and began passing out weapons " -- The Journey of Tansek

name: Domesticate
rules_text: In the Tan'u'zhadhi Willow analogy, the original will of the creature is called "paint on a hidden canvas".

name: Civilize
rules_text: When this structure creates any novices, those novices are eliminated.

name: Sword of Zana
rules_text: Zana called her generals and blessed their swords. "It is a terrible power I give you," she said. "But when Vask falls, all this land will be free of horror."

name: Pyrrhic Victory
rules_text: "The Elves may sing of the beauty of life and the light in the trees -- to me there is no poetry like the poetry of justice." -- Sayings of Pantogar

name: Venom'd Arrow
rules_text: A shaft of ebony and fletched with vulturine feathers; it seeks out the victim, and guides him gently through the Lesser and Greater Deaths.

name: Dryad's Favors
rules_text: Dryads are peaceful folk who rely on seduction and guile. Those bewitched by dryads often disappear for days, returning with no memory of what happened.

name: Oasis
rules_text: "At last we reached Shederef. One can hardly believe our folk live here, but hidden in the barren crevasses is a lush world of green." -- Journal of Lienna: Volume XI

name: Valiant Stand
rules_text: "Perhaps there was no error, but only my Fate; perhaps I will win the contest  and my errors were in the end a path to glory." -- Zaduth-Jar, Lecturer in Xenology

name: Faerie Fire
rules_text: "Flee! The arrows sting with light! And we are lost!" -- Bilegnash Tarboil, Imp

name: Werewolf
rules_text: Territorial.

name: Binding Cube
rules_text: "We sought the walls that held them in place and found none. Finally we had to leave them. They weren't imprisoned -- they but thought themselves to be " -- Dwarven scout's report

name: Sentinel
rules_text: "The Gargoyle turned his silent face towards us. His Dwarf spoke. 'He commands us to stay,' said Ateros, 'until their arrows pierce our souls.'" -- Diary of Vlad Hrothgar

name: Pixie Dust
rules_text: "Pixies are the tiniest of Nature's minions, but a handful in your hair are enough to incapacitate your entire company." -- Uyden's Handbook

name: Albatross
rules_text:
The many men, so beautiful
and they all dead did lie -- and
a thousand, thousand slimy things
lived on -- and so did I.

-- Samuel Taylor Coleridge,
"The Rime of the Ancient Mariner"

name: Mechanical Man
rules_text: Nomadic. Two attacks. Withdraws at the start of combat round 3.

name: Revenants
rules_text: Nomadic.

name: Skeleton
rules_text: Nomadic. Receives -2 damage from missiles (before checking armor).

name: Ancient King
rules_text: Ancient King is a swordsman. Ancient King gets +1 hand damage, +1 armor, and +2 maximum and current hit points. When he dies, all surviving members of his group get Legacy (+1 level).

name: Water Walking
rules_text: "Ugly meathead crossed the stream without a splash. Never bet with a Cyclops in mating season!" -- Stonewallow, Satyr

name: Strength of Body
rules_text: "Oooh  muscles!" -- Elf Chicks from Hell, Radical Misfit Order

name: Ascension
rules_text: "The sky opened up and took her, laughing, from us. My comrades fell to their knees. 'Take us too, Zana! We are worthy!'" -- The Tale of Bella

name: Armor to Meat
rules_text: "Before this spell, Dwarves so tinny and unpalatable. Now can eat five at a sitting!" -- Seppukarno, Manticore

name: Bow of Quickening
rules_text: Originally commissioned for Auberol's use in the Elven lands, this bow was quickly adopted by the Guildsmen who made it.

name: Rain of Arrows
rules_text: "Humans dispense with accuracy in favor of speed and quantity of arrows. An inelegant approach, but an effective one." -- Turgan Qef, Kumatru Academy

name: Berserkers
rules_text: "They cut through our ranks of soldiers with fearsome speed, their eyes rolling madly in their blood-spattered faces, drunk with rage." -- Account of an Elven archer, Battle of Thurmagol

name: Bolt of Somersaults
rules_text: "Can we do it again? Can we please, can we?" -- Elevava Glasshead, Misfit traveler

name: Disorient
rules_text: "We seem to drift, the whole of our company seized with a dull panic at once. We have wandered the hills for days now." -- Diary of Vlad Hrothgar

name: Amok
rules_text: Among Humans, the state of Amok is considered almost holy. Lieutenants of War maintain that sheer berserker rage can often accomplish more than cool strategy.

name: Stalking Blyk
rules_text: Nomadic. Two attacks.

name: Trolls
rules_text: Territorial.

name: Man of Iron
rules_text: Nomadic.

name: Obsidian Dragon
rules_text: Recruit gets +1 armor for having helped its group to kill an Obsidian Dragon.

name: Manticore
rules_text: Territorial. Withdraws at the start of combat round 3.

name: Tree Man
rules_text: Territorial.

name: Banshee
rules_text: Territorial. At start of combat, kills one random opposing minion.

name: Changelings
rules_text: Recruit gets -1 hand and missile damage and -3 maximum hit points because it was created in a structure with Changelings.

name: Disintegrate
rules_text: "Entropy unleashed, the whirligig dance -- hey hey! What fun to see a fellow fall apart from inside out!" -- ThudThud, Misfit, late of the Kumatru Academy

name: Lightning Bolt
rules_text: "The skies themselves roiled at our approach, and then the great lightning struck not far away " -- Kyman Firth, "The Siege of Groven's Weald"

name: Fireball
rules_text: It starts as a spark, cupped in the Mage's hands

name: Healing Spring
rules_text: "He struck the ground with his Dythramb, laughing, and a spring of fresh water immediately bubbled forth, wholesome and clear." -- Kyman Firth, "The Siege of Groven's Weald"

name: Minion of Order
rules_text: "I act under sanction of Law; submit or be compelled to comply." -- Ateros, Dwarven agent of the Justicars

name: Regenerate
rules_text: "Do not resist the hurt; it is in you. Sing to torn flesh, be it, be the cracked bone. Be the body. When words are gone, heal." -- Song of Xia

name: Bog Horror
rules_text: Territorial. At start of combat, casts Plague (-2 maximum hit points, then 2 hit points of damage) on opposing group.

name: Vicious Strength
rules_text: "Odar thought he saw Ogi before him, and he struck with a dragon's force; then his madness cleared, and he saw it was his own son's head rolling on the tiles." -- The Ballad of Odar

name: Water Breathing
rules_text: Under the protection of the Naiads, travelers may move silently through their underwater grottos.

name: Swamp Land
rules_text: Group in swamp can exit only in the direction from which any group most recently entered that swamp.

name: Mountain
rules_text: Mountain blocks movement unless group has Mountainwalk.

name: Plains
rules_text: "Leave the enemy no cover, and nowhere to go to ground." -- Ngozi, The Book of War

name: Inundate
rules_text: Each minion drowns (dies) in water when water is created, when minion enters square, after start of turn, before movement, and before combat, unless minion has Waterwalk. Water blocks movement unless group has Waterwalk.

name: Forestation
rules_text: Minions in forest are concealed. (Concealed minions cannot be targeted by enemy individual spells.)

name: Mountain Pass
rules_text: With insubstantial hope, heroes can tumble the walls of fortresses and move mountains. It will open any impasse effortlessly, even that which seems set in stone.

name: Void
rules_text: Void removes group from the game when Void is created, when group enters square, after start of turn, before movement, and before combat. (This does not trigger any death effects.) Void does not block movement.

name: Volcano
rules_text: Volcano causes 5 hit points of damage after start of turn unless group has Firewalk. Volcano blocks movement unless group has Mountainwalk.

name: Ogi's Armor
rules_text: Many were stunned when Ogi broke from the mighty Armorers' Guild, founded by Beobogh himself. But Ogi's control over the upstart Fletchers' Guild was absolute, which served his purposes.

name: Flaming Sword
rules_text: "The Flaming Sword is to be wielded by a great hero. Cowards will only burn themselves." -- Ngozi, The Book of War

name: Weakness
rules_text: Chaotic deformation of musculature, tissue collapse and painful cramping are the first gifts Unmaking grants the seeker.

name: Body Rot
rules_text: Discoveries in embalming, the Queen of Keeper sciences, sometimes have interesting military applications

name: Healing Balm
rules_text: Cyclops medicinal herbology is largely unknown outside of their tribes, as it has never been written down. Their healing balm, Xexos, is legendary.

name: Ngozi's Way
rules_text: "Seek to be as your sword is: sharp, supple, deadly, and with a single point." -- Ngozi, The Book of War

name: Stupefy
rules_text: "He saw there the Shadow which had beset him for years, source of all his agony. He raised his axe; but its face was his own, and he faltered " -- The Ballad of Odar

name: Rain of Blood
rules_text: Ritual genocide is the aim of every true Mage of Abomination. Each living soul he kills feeds the engines of his unholy power.

name: Legionnaires
rules_text: The elite fighting force of the Human Nations, the Legionnaires, are practiced in the arts of swordsmanship and strategy.

name: Shieldbearers
rules_text: In the hope of winning honor and a bride, younger sons of the elite Guilds will sometimes insist on serving as footsoldiers. Every precaution is taken to ensure their safety.

name: Skeletal Horror
rules_text: Territorial, Mountainwalk. Receives -2 damage from missiles (before checking armor).

name: Spirit Sword
rules_text: When first you take up the Blade of Clarity, its light burns away all your doubts and fears, that you may strike true.

name: Accursed Minion
rules_text: The Chosen One of Ulanoth carries the seed of Abomination. If he suspects what is within him, he does not tell his fellows

name: Retribution
rules_text: Those who dare strike the emissary of Order face swift and terrible reprisal at the hands of the just.

name: Dragon's Teeth
rules_text: In the Song of Khulainn, the hero sowed dragon's teeth on the site of what is now Olmstead Marsh, and a host of new soldiers sprang up from the clay.

name: Cannibalism
rules_text: "How I tire of the hardtack we're served! I would do just about anything for some nice, fresh meat." -- Letter from the Omistad front

name: Bella's Sigil
rules_text: Monster cannot move.

name: Kumatru Trance
rules_text: Kumatru Academy, 9th year subjects: Trance, Metaontology, Epistemic Projection, Cosmology, Purity, Stochastic Projection, Esthetics/Ethics, Emanative Catastrophics.

name: Whipping Boy
rules_text: "When you're backed into a corner, start looking for a Whipping Boy." -- Impish Proverb

name: Spawn of Toganni
rules_text:
And with what fear my soul is rent
Seeing Toganni's Spawn descend
Upon whose judgment all depend!
-- Human epic poem, Farthrad band

name: Mirrored Armor
rules_text: "At first, seeing all those perspectives is exhilarating, but after a while you almost hope the soulless thing will break " -- Elven mercenary in Sar'ul Academy employ

name: Mark of Ulanoth
rules_text: "Your arrows will find no purchase in Ulanoth's chosen " -- Maloch

name: Betrayal
rules_text: The Tan'u'zhadhi Academy specializes in the study of the will and the mind. Its less profound members concentrate on manipulating wills and minds

name: Codex of Order
rules_text: By the naming and describing of all things known and unknown, the Justicars bring Order to all they survey.

name: Huntsman
rules_text: Territorial. Gets +2 hand damage against monsters.

name: Dracha's Sphere
rules_text: Nothing shall impede the ministers of Justice in the swift execution of their appointed tasks.

name: Forsaken
rules_text: "All gates are closed against me, all men turn their eyes from me, wherever I go the wind mocks me for my sins." -- The Ballad of Odar

name: Master Smith
rules_text: Swordsman gets +1 hand damage because it was created in a structure with Master Smith.

name: Master Bowyer
rules_text: Archer gets +1 missile damage because it was created in a structure with Master Bowyer.

name: Kumatru Archer
rules_text: "Those schools disdain our empirical focus, but at times like this they appreciate our precision." --Turgan Qef, Kumatru Academy

name: Gifts of the Fallen
rules_text: "I had served with that Legion before. Though the faces were new, I recognized a few stitched-on hands and feet of former comrades. The Keepers are economical." -- Diary of Vlad Hrothgar

name: Necromancer
rules_text: "'Apologies, brother,' Bes-Kandra said, reaching down to the common soldier's corpse. 'I cannot let you sleep just yet.'" -- Diary of Sharul-Nar

name: Astronomic Clock
rules_text: The Dwarven understanding and conquest of time has made their nation a marvel of efficiency and progress.

name: Nomadic Tribe
rules_text: "Your pulse is the pulse of the world, and will drum on in your progeny." -- Respectful ritual greeting among the Cyclopes

name: Limbo
rules_text: "The land dissolved. In the whirling mists I caught strange glimpses of a place I did not know; but I was sure I had been there before, if only in a horrid dream " -- Jan Palczred, mercenary

name: The Star Chamber
rules_text: Those who have appeared before the Star Council rarely relate their experiences. In fact, they rarely return.

name: Ritual Cleansing
rules_text: An individual afflicted with a Mala Ozo must go on walkabout away from the tribe, taking with her only her drum.

name: Restoration
rules_text: Dracha grants you sanction to lift unwanted enchantments, but it will cost you.

name: Cleansing Light
rules_text: "I felt the power seep from my bones as we looked to the sky to see the source of the light. Only afterwards did we realize that both our gifts were gone." -- Elven captain

name: Nullify
rules_text: Among the Djinni Academies, the Xin Shia distinguish themselves by their fascination with paradox, contradiction, and reversals.

name: Found City
rules_text: Few understand the processes involved in creating a town from nothing, fewer still know the hidden workings of the Dwarves below ground.

name: Army of Light
rules_text: As inspiration grows and spreads, fledgling whispered hopes become stronger, substantial Visions.

name: Possession
rules_text: "My hearthbrother, Ruldar, was among the madmen beating at the gate, frothing at the mouth and shrieking obscenities. I believe our arrows were merciful." -- Account of the Siege of Omistad

name: Lienna's Sigil
rules_text: "The beast's heart had always been clouded by rage. This life in you, I told it silently, is the same life as is in me." -- Journal of Lienna: Volume XII

name: Arcane Stigma
rules_text: Oh, those Imps! They do get into trouble, and when they do, Arcane Stigma is there to take care of it!

name: Xia's Walk
rules_text: Singing this song of Xia allows the shaman to traverse long distances hidden in the Cessao ("ancestors' realm"). The Cyclopes say the Cessao exists as the negative side of the physical world.

name: Ogi's Gauntlet
rules_text: "Theriel didn't die in battle. He died for talking of Ogi and Adriel's tryst to the wrong person. The Kingmaker was serious about covering his tracks." -- Seelie court gossip

name: Chaos Fog
rules_text: Fingle's attempt to recreate his Blinding Orb for the Misfits who took him in. For this he was given the Impractical Joke Pickle, honor and insult.

name: Inscrutability
rules_text: "The Tan'u'zhadhi fool you, the Kumatru fool the light, and I gather those Xin Shia fool themselves -- Djinn are sneakier than Imps, and no fit allies for this Hearth!" -- Heard in a Dwarven High Council

name: Reanimate
rules_text: "Back again, out of the sweet, dark night into the jittery light of twitching pump-bloodies!" -- Keeper Moan

name: Mirage
rules_text: "What joy, what splendor! The never-can, the wasn't-once, the sweet escape of is-not-is " -- Xin Shia Academy sage

name: Demon's Tongue
rules_text: Who wields the Demon's Tongue, the Eater of Souls, feeds off the pain of those he kills. If he knew how he will pay, later, he might pause.

name: False Prophet
rules_text: This spell brings Ekrivan, the silver-tongued demon. He binds the energies of its citizens to false and fruitless pursuits. Forever.

name: Fountain of Youth
rules_text: Recruit gets -1 level for having entered a Fountain of Youth.

name: Plague
rules_text: The Plague is kept alive carefully in a special tomb in Bes-Tharal. A constant supply of slaves must be infected and imprisoned there  And they must never escape.

name: Golem
rules_text: Nomadic. Receives -1 damage from swordsmen (before checking armor).

name: Harrowing Cry
rules_text: The cries of the Grey Shadows are themselves weapons, sapping the wills of soldiers and paralyzing them with fear.

name: Shaman's Walk
rules_text: Xa Macao spell of the highest order. The shaman with strong Xa Macao can walk her tribe across the abyss.

name: Parted Sea
rules_text: The Well of Souls is said to be guarded by eight great seas that part for the Nameless Ones, and four impassable mountains whose secret paths they know.

name: Prophet
rules_text: When a great soul walks the world, his light crosses all boundaries; that which enlightens is ineffable and not restricted to nations.

name: Sanctuary
rules_text: That which the Armies of Hope claim as Sacred Ground, none may pollute with bloodshed.

name: Kumatru Library
rules_text: The Kumatru fondness for physical books is considered atavistic by most Djinn.

name: Caravan
rules_text: "The driver knew the secrets of desert. The scarred man led the line of animals and men through the night, avoiding even the most devious of pitfalls." -- Journal of Lienna: Volume XI

name: Haunted Forest
rules_text: Gallowswode timber, prized by Elves for its fragrance, is a great rarity  Few woodcutters enter such forests, and fewer still return.

name: Primeval Forest
rules_text: Monster gets +1 hand damage because it was created in a Primeval Forest square.

name: Ill Winds
rules_text: The Winds Ventrius, Borundo, Celyph, and Autrenith are the oldest and mightiest of the Unseelie Horde.

name: Bleak Isle
rules_text: Group gains Waterwalk because it was in a structure when Bleak Isle was cast on it. (Group may cross water, and does not drown in water.)

name: Fortitude
rules_text: Rooted in the world as it is, cradled in this moment, the shaman's disciples are free of pain and fear when the foe's blade bites.

name: Redeemer's Sword
rules_text: "These swords are infused with the life of the world itself. They are said to have been created in the time of the world's greatest need." -- Uyden's Handbook

name: Leadership
rules_text: The hero who walks with vision inspires love and courage in his fellows. The more who partake of the spark of vision, the stronger it becomes.

name: Faerie Circle
rules_text: Minion gets +1 level for having visited a Faerie Circle. Minion's group no longer disappears in Faerie Circle.

name: Berserker Blade
rules_text: When the rage is on him, a Human soldier is said to be "steeped in blood". They say in his rage Khulainn spouted fire from his head thirty feet into the sky.

name: Prismatic Serpent
rules_text: Nomadic. Gets +4 hand damage against Death or Despair monsters. Cannot be targeted by Death or Despair individual spells.

name: Pact of Mystery
rules_text: "I wish I were at liberty to discuss our  arrangement with the Justicars." -- Zaduth-Jar, Lecturer in Xenology

name: The Unmagicking
rules_text: "For every enchantment that fell, two shall take its place. Ours will be the last enchantments." - Fingle, the Fallen

name: Pages to Dust
rules_text: Who will be left standing when the season of magic ends, and the weighty tomes are sand and ash?

name: Sylph
rules_text: Territorial. Two attacks. Receives no combat damage from recruits without enchantments (after checking armor).

name: Call to Arms
rules_text: "A nation once again, A nation once again " -- Battle-Hymn of Valgrave

name: Citadel
rules_text: The Parcae, inner circle of Justice, are hidden at the hub of an elaborate and secret infrastructure, while their silent agents walk the outer world.

name: Ring of Light
rules_text: Twenty-four summers are required for a full recitation of the Clarity Cycle, and the town so honored is sometimes hard-pressed to keep the Bards in luxury that long.

name: Necropolis
rules_text: A mortal whose bodily systems are failing as a result of starvation, suffocation or loss of fluids, particularly if unwilling, is considered to be the ideal state to apprehend Mystery.

name: Will to Power
rules_text: "There was my master, my teacher, torn almost to shreds by our knives. Our eyes locked; I gazed into his bottomless hatred, and knew joy." -- the Infernalist Javhakr

name: World Dance
rules_text: "How pale and sickly your clarity looks in comparison, when the Diomesian throng of revelers sweeps past!" -- Acornbelly, boasting to Lienna

name: Cenotaph of Will
rules_text: "The true struggle is each mortal being's struggle to impose her will permanently upon the Universe. This struggle is always doomed, and because it is doomed, it is beautiful." -- Bes-Kandra

name: Aura of World
rules_text: Through clarity of purpose, and openness of heart, the Elven mage embraces the fullness of the world, as the warm night embraces the firelit dance in a midsummer's grove.

name: Threshold of Order
rules_text: "Step forward, through fear. Fear cannot limit you. All is as it should be in the world; know this, and be free of the chains of mystery and strife." -- Sayings of Zana to the Acolytes

name: Augur of Strife
rules_text: The last words Pantogar wrote were, "Each day that my eyes are open on this world is a torture to endure."

name: Balance of Mystery
rules_text: The shaman guides the tribe's life: when to move, to camp, to hunt, to dance. The future is unknown: each step is into mystery; each day the shaman is wiser, and knows less.

name: Focus of Clarity
rules_text: "So he juxtaposed the Inverted Endless and the root of the convexity of sums!" -- punch line to a Djinni joke

name: Carapace
rules_text: "Does not mutation provide? We become the rules, hide our blood in cornhusks, rubber, and salty flint! And only bruise ourselves!" -- Mangrove Onyx the Bloody Icicle, Misfit demagogue

name: Jaguar's Blood
rules_text: He who bathes in the Jaguar's Blood takes on the vigor and spirit of the great cat.

name: Shell of Gold
rules_text: Created by two Master Smiths, this golden skin was crafted for its beauty. It has replaced the bulkier Mail of Lead for isolating and dispelling magics.

name: True Aim
rules_text: Where negotiations break down, Order may come on the point of an arrow.

name: Pantogar's Curse
rules_text: The Djinn Pantogar developed a passion for Justice, and shut himself away to complete a Book of Law. He was found years later, shrieking, his manuscript an unintelligible ramble.

name: Hero's Legacy
rules_text: Recruit gets +1 level from a Hero's Legacy.

name: Settlement
rules_text: "With a song in your hearts strike down the wilderness, clear land and plant new seed. Cultivate Hope, and prepare to reap a new harvest." -- Sayings of Zana to the Acolytes

name: Farm-Boys
rules_text: Recruit gets -1 level because it was created in a structure with Farm-Boys.

name: Insight
rules_text: "To vanquish your enemy's deceit is not difficult. Deceit is brittle. The difficulty lies in your own unwillingness to see." -- Al Hakim

name: Minotaur
rules_text: Swordsman gets +1 hand damage for having helped its group to kill a Minotaur.

name: Boggart
rules_text: Nomadic.

name: Lycanthropy
rules_text: Group is player-controlled. If group is dispelled, each Wolf is replaced by a new recruit of the original nation and class, with the same amount of damage. (This does not trigger any death effects.)

name: Yfreet
rules_text: Territorial. At start of combat, casts Cleansing Light (group dispel) on opposing group.

name: Pegasus
rules_text: Nomadic, Flight, Moves 2. Receives -1 damage from minions without Flight (before checking armor).

name: Giant
rules_text: Nomadic, Mountainwalk. At start of combat, throws rock (5 hit points of damage) at one random opposing minion.

name: Celestial Sphere
rules_text: Territorial, Flight.

name: Maloch Horror
rules_text: Nomadic, Flight.

name: Brimstone Dragon
rules_text: Territorial, Flight. At start of combat, casts Fireball (5 hit points of damage) on opposing group.

name: Pyx
rules_text: Cherished by troops in battle, the healing salve contained within has saved more than one Elven life.

name: Apocalypse
rules_text: "The final wrath, delight of my lord Khobai, the conflagration in which all are blasted, forgotten." -- Zharduk Thal

name: Burst of World
rules_text: The shaman calls upon the World Snake for power.

name: Burst of Will
rules_text: "Fool Elves tie me by one arm only! As if the chew through arm not in me! For Master's pleasure and Elf meat, I chew each my arm!" -- Painscreech Boilsuck, Imp

name: Burst of Order
rules_text: An investment in Guild magic is seldom for naught.

name: Burst of Strife
rules_text: The romance of decay, the intrigue of mutation, the glory of anarchy and the triumphant silence of rot. The end is always Strife. Always. And sometimes the beginning too.

name: Burst of Clarity
rules_text: Clarity showers upon the world, enriching everything with its brilliance.

name: Burst of Mystery
rules_text: "As if learning more would do more than poison me for that moment when I face the Bone-white Fathers, and all is swept away!" -- Zaduth-Jar, Lecturer in Xenology

name: Will o' the Wisp
rules_text: "Finally on the third day they came stumbling over the hill. We asked them where they had been. 'We followed a light,' they told us." -- Diary of Vlad Hrothgar

name: Skirmish
rules_text: "Choose your battles wisely; know when to retreat, as well as when to strike." -- Ngozi, The Book of War

name: Belvario's Trap
rules_text: Designed by the late Guild Master to trap the Great Bear of Cyclopean folklore. He lived long enough to see the trap in action, but never imagined the fury of the wounded beast

name: Wasteland
rules_text:
Suck the spit out --
not dry enough.
Suck the blood out --
not dry enough.
Crush bone brittle, scattered dry.
Thousand years, empty sky.
-- Keeper children's chant

name: Blinding Orb
rules_text: Fingle invented and activated his Blinding Orb to cover the Dwarves' retreat from Arboria. Since Fingle's banishment, it is used by Dwarves without attribution.

name: Ogi's Arrows
rules_text: "After Thrgal, Patriarch Odar was forced to give the Fletchers full autonomy. Hana'udhi models Dwarven politics using parastatic dominance tensors " -- Report of the Djinni ambassador

name: Pestilence
rules_text: The first wave of victims bloats with a putrid stench, vomits black bile, and dies slowly and painfully. Survivors are rendered sterile.

name: Nereid's Curse
rules_text: A Xa Macao ("world walking") spell. To elude their enemies, some Cyclops tribes grew gills and took to the lakes. From then on, the dryness of the land burned them.

name: Mountain Climb
rules_text: A Xa Macao ("world walking") spell. Well-trained shamen can run up and down sheer cliff faces at will.

name: Intercession
rules_text: All must submit to the implacable mystery of Law.

name: Flight
rules_text: "The archers turned in midair to offer us a hail of arrows; a deadly maneuver indeed." -- Turgan Qef, Kumatru Academy

name: Ostralek
rules_text: Ostralek is a Hero swordsman. At start of turn, he kills the most damaged other recruit in his group and gets +1 hand damage and +2 maximum and current hit points. Cannot be targeted by Cannibalism. (Each Hero may be cast only once per game.)

name: Entrail Eater
rules_text: Nomadic. At start of turn, Entrail Eater is fully healed.

name: Rogue Monster
rules_text: Twists the mind of the monster to madness, until it lashes out at all around it.

name: Rampage
rules_text: Monster gets +1 attack rate and +5 maximum and current hit points because it was created in a square with Rampage.

name: Wild Plague
rules_text: "We watched from the gates as the manticore devoured the screaming slave. 'The plague is out now,' I told Varnash-Zul. 'And it will run its course.'" -- Diary of Sharul-Nar

name: Revelation
rules_text: "Time is not an illusion; everything else is " -- Al Hakim

name: Dark Minion
rules_text: The Keeper communication network, the Dark Whispering, functions wherever there are graves.

name: Blindness
rules_text: Even the fiercest creature can be blinded by a blighted heart.

name: Chalice of Hope
rules_text: "This chalice we seek may be only a dream, but then the creatures of Hope are dreams too in their way." -- Journal of Lienna: Volume III

name: Terrain Bind
rules_text: "'Ah, you do not love my Lady, Justicar,' a silver voice whispered from the trees. 'Then love the ground you stand on, and love it well.'" -- Ballad of the Justicar

name: Karillian Arrows
rules_text: Satyr archers cultivate the poison from the glands of these venomous Karillian tree frogs; they use a distillate to tip their arrows.

name: Bard
rules_text: Bard is a swordsman. Bard gets +2 armor, and does not deal hand or missile damage. Other swordsmen in Bard's group get +1 hand damage.

name: Ostracize
rules_text: A penalty of Justicar adjudication. Any group so condemned is shunned among nations. Even the Misfits will not take them in.

name: Ogi
rules_text: Ogi is a Hero archer. Ogi has Ogi's Armor, Ogi's Arrows, and Ogi's Gauntlet. These spells have no casting cost for Ogi's caster while Ogi is in play. (Each Hero may be cast only once per game.)

name: Zana
rules_text: Zana is a Hero swordswoman. While Zana is in play, all monsters join her caster's side (but keep previous control by computer or player). (Each Hero may be cast only once per game.)

name: Al Hakim
rules_text: Al Hakim is a Hero swordsman. At start of turn, Al Hakim tells his caster the next three cards at the top of his caster's deck (after caster has drawn cards this turn), and all cards in the opponent's hand (before opponent draws cards this turn). (Each Hero may be cast only once per game.)

name: Khobai
rules_text: Khobai is a Hero swordsman. Each time a minion dies, Khobai heals 1 hit point. If undamaged, Khobai also gets +1 maximum hit point. (Each Hero may be cast only once per game.)

name: Karkara
rules_text: When this spell is dispelled, Karkara is removed from the game, and its target recruit group reappears, with all damage and spells intact, and that group executes Karkara's movement order that turn. (This does not trigger any death effects.) If Karkara dies, its target recruit group is removed from the game.

name: Ring of Fire
rules_text: "Look not to the right or left; you are surrounded by the Nine Hells." -- the Infernalist Javhakr

name: Changing Lands
rules_text: These roiling turbulent areas are thought to spontaneously create Misfits. And perhaps that is why Misfits create Changing Lands wherever they can.

name: Ngozi
rules_text: Ngozi is a Hero swordswoman. Before movement, and when Ngozi moves, the four squares adjacent to her group become barren land. ("Moves" effects happen before any "enters square" effects.) (Each Hero may be cast only once per game.)

name: Larval Imp
rules_text: Imp larvae lie dormant in a host for years. The putrefaction of dead flesh feeds them and they burst forth, fully grown and covered with entrails.

name: Kumatru Academy
rules_text: Kumatru Academy, 7th year subjects: Focus, Teleontology, Convergence, Rhetoric, Silence, Tactics, Analysis of Forms, Emanative Dynamics.

name: Adriel's Timepiece
rules_text: Elven women kept the Dwarven nation from extinction after the Great Wasting. This gift to Adriel, the Elven Protectress of Life, was created soon after.

name: Siege Engine
rules_text: Engines of this type are often crafted to resemble Dragons, Rocs and other giant monsters. The engineers cleave to natural forms, knowing the fear and confusion this can create.

name: Lode Star
rules_text: The last project Fingle completed before his exile was intended to draw metal from the heavens, but proved more sensible for defense. Some Guilds seek to ban its use.

name: Fingle
rules_text: Fingle is a Hero swordsman. Every player-controlled group in the eight squares surrounding Fingle moves in a random legal direction. (Each Hero may be cast only once per game.)

name: Fingle's Folly
rules_text: Nomadic. Neutral. Four attacks. If Fingle's Folly dies in combat, it casts Fireball (5 hit points of damage) on opposing group.

name: Leechwood
rules_text: This eerie creature is nourished by enchantment. The more magic it devours the blacker it becomes, whispering a low dissonant gurgle.

name: Jumping Land
rules_text: "A little wanderlust is good for those stodgy mountainy things and -- hey trala! -- for their overlords in House of Making." -- Ovo, Brass of the Sky

name: Salamander
rules_text: Lava causes 3 hit points of damage after start of turn unless group has Firewalk.

name: Dragon Slayer
rules_text: Khulainn, the great hero of the Northern Nations, slew an ancient Obsidian Dragon at Olmstead. To this day, "Son of Khulainn" is a title of respect conferred upon great archers.

name: Renegades
rules_text: "Humans. They are constantly splitting into factions, nursing small grievances, rising up and killing one another. What's the point?" -- The Journey of Tansek

name: Dracha
rules_text: Dracha is a Hero swordsman. Cannot be targeted by individual spells. When he dies, he creates a friendly colony if his square is empty, and his group withdraws at the start of the next round, ending combat. (Each Hero may be cast only once per game.)

name: Ebon Guardian
rules_text: Immobile. Two attacks. (Immobile monsters cannot be moved.)

name: Justicars' Reserve
rules_text: Ancient stones demarcate the permitted Ground. While the Court of Being is in session, no creature, however mad, will respond to a Mage's call from proscribed territory.

name: Undine
rules_text: Immobile. Plains, forest, desert, and barren land in the four squares adjacent to Undine attract non-friendly player-controlled groups. (Groups adjacent to attracting squares before movement must move toward one of them.) Undine dies if not in water. (Immobile monsters cannot be moved.)

name: Nihil
rules_text: Nihil is a Hero swordsman. Cannot join groups or add recruits. When hit by a minion for 1 or more damage (before checking armor), the attacking minion gets Nihil's Curse (cumulative, -3 hand and missile damage, regains +1 damage per turn for 3 turns). (Each Hero may be cast only once per game.)

name: Gorgon
rules_text: Swordsman's hits give Gorgon's Gaze (-1 damage and +1 armor, cumulative. A minion who gets three or more Gorgon's Gaze is killed). Gorgon's Head transfers to a swordsman who kills its owner.

name: Lienna
rules_text: Lienna is a Hero archer. While Lienna is in play, her caster's side has Initiative. (When Lienna leaves play, her caster's side does not automatically lose Initiative.) (Each Hero may be cast only once per game.)

name: Unicorn Herd
rules_text: Recruit is set to level 0 and cannot gain levels for having killed a Unicorn.

name: Incarnate
rules_text: Elvish peasants often name their daughters Lienna in the hope of "catching her spirit". While the elite find this crass, the court, who know Lienna did not die, find it hilarious.

name: Siren Song
rules_text: "I am your torment, your destruction, and the cold fire of your longing. Never more will you know hearth and beloved, but only suffering and oblivion in my embrace. Come to me " -- Adoni, Merrow

name: Sand Sphinx
rules_text: Nomadic, Desertwalk. Sand Sphinx seeks opponent's Sanctum. When Sand Sphinx moves, its new square becomes desert. (Desert causes 1 hit point of damage after start of turn unless group has Desertwalk.) ("Moves" effects happen before any "enters square" effects.)

name: Touch of Death
rules_text: It is a great honor to be endowed with the Touch, and the warrior who has it is called 'The Generous'. Not all foes appreciate his generosity.

name: Olotus
rules_text: Olotus is a Hero swordsman. When Olotus is cast, all Cyclopes on the board (including Olotus) get Rite of Growth (+1 hand damage and +4 maximum and current hit points). (Each Hero may be cast only once per game.)

name: Priodont
rules_text: Territorial, Desertwalk.

name: Bear Spirit
rules_text: In this ritual the warrior takes on the spirit of the bear. Voda Ursa is a powerful totem animal among the Cyclopes.

name: Loch Dweller
rules_text: Three attacks. Before combat, Loch Dweller appears in a water square, or a square adjacent to a friendly town or colony, that is also adjacent to a non-friendly group. After all combat, it disappears again. Withdraws at the start of combat round 3. Dispelled when killed.

name: Diomesia
rules_text: Diomesia is a Hero swordswoman. All swordsmen in her group and any opposing group do not deal hand damage. (Each Hero may be cast only once per game.)

name: Simian Warriors
rules_text: Territorial.

name: Flying Carpet
rules_text: "Best value Carpets of Zin! Guaranteed not to unravel!" -- Djinni sign in a Human bazaar

name: Zana's Blessing
rules_text: "The terrible foe confronted us before the grime-encrusted walls of Vask, and we were sore afraid. Then we heard voices lifted in song and the whine of arrows, and we knew that our prayers had been answered." -- Alicar, Elven bard

name: Freeze
rules_text: When group enters ice, group slides in its direction of travel to the first non-ice square, or the first square that blocks movement. A sliding group ignores other groups in all squares except the last square, and all spells in squares except the first ice square and the last square. Flight does not prevent sliding.

name: Citizens' Militia
rules_text: "I despise the arrogance of would-be tyrants; underestimate me, and I will spit in your eye." -- Ngozi's Manifesto

name: City-State
rules_text: Under the cold, unblinking gaze of the Gargoyles, martial law is swiftly and efficiently enforced.

name: Fertility
rules_text: The control the Cyclops tribe exercises over its own reproduction indicates a powerful herbal pharmacology. Childless for long treks with few resources, a tribe will seemingly reproduce overnight when occasion demands.

name: Stone Circle
rules_text: The harmonics of living Stone can be used by the clever Geomancer. Dwarven stonesmiths have tried to duplicate the effect through engineering, with no success.

name: Geomancy
rules_text: There have ever been cycles of climatic change. Geomancy simply hastens Nature's abundant renewal.

name: Volgaris
rules_text: Nomadic. Two attacks. At start of combat, casts Lightning Bolt (5 hit points of damage) on one random opposing minion.

name: Husks
rules_text: Nomadic, can join other Husks.

name: Sawbones
rules_text: Sawbones is a swordsman. At end of combat, if any other members of its group are damaged, Sawbones kills the most damaged other recruit in its group, and all surviving members of its group are fully healed.

name: Putrefaction
rules_text: "Ostralek was the first of them. His hatred of Nature was boundless. His first act was a curse that struck the wild wood silent and littered the forest floor with corpses." -- the Saga of Goldleaf

name: Djibari's Legacy
rules_text: "What then does the Sar'ul Academy teach, if not extremes? To become a Misfit feared by Misfits, to corrupt the gentle Satyrs -- why do Djibari's acts embarrass the Sar'uli? He but had the courage of their convictions!" -- Ch'tai Academy polemic

name: Gogar's Wrath
rules_text: "In bubble boil bile pool Gogar writhes. Spite his delight, and crushing hate. Let Gogar ruin what you used to like!" -- Spleen Bloodboil, Imp thaumaturge and merchant

name: Bilious Rage
rules_text: "You say this is Nature's Design, that I die of her plague? Then Nature will no more be my love! I find I prize my own self above her! Tell me, friend Djibari, what must I do?" -- Goldleaf the Satyr, father of the Imps

name: Blue Auk
rules_text: Nomadic,  Waterwalk.

name: Great Bear
rules_text: Nomadic. Cannot be targeted by Mind spells. In combat against Djinni, Great Bear does a "combined attack" (always targets the first opposing Djinn, and each attack hits all opposing Djinni simultaneously).

name: Cessao Rift
rules_text: Every journey that has a beginning, has an end. Shamen on their dreamwalks discovered the secret to the other side of the Void, and the greatest of them can open the door from this side.

name: Immersion
rules_text: When her daughter Sibiki was killed by wild beasts, Xia cried for seven years. Her tears fell into the Sacred Lake, and whoever bathes there will escape Sibiki's fate.

name: Bellwether
rules_text: Bellwether is a swordsman. Bellwether does not attack. Its group cannot be targeted by enemy group spells.

name: Ancestral Home
rules_text: "Before there were stars or birds or wind or rain, out of the ocean came Grandfather World-Walker. On his back was the first village, and there sat the first four of our people who ever lived." -- Cyclops legend

name: Mask of Woad
rules_text: "The monoculars may be mindless, as the Sar'uli claim, but their souls are the mirror of ours, their silence the mirror of our thoughts. Whatever they suffer at our hands, we will surely suffer in return, for no jailer is ever truly free." -- Ch'tai Academy polemic

name: Master Vampire
rules_text: Territorial. Two attacks. Gets +1 hand damage against Life minions. At end of combat, if Master Vampire survives and any member of its group killed a recruit by combat damage, one Novice Vampire (H:3 A:0 HP:5 L:1 Territorial) is created in group.

name: Furies
rules_text: Nomadic, Flight. At start of combat, each Fury kills one random opposing minion, and all Furies in this group are removed from the game, ending combat. (This does not trigger any death effects for the Furies.)

name: Curse of Khobai
rules_text: "Lord Khobai had discovered my treachery, and for an endless night I waited in terror. Then, for the first time, I saw my Master smile. It was a grisly smile." -- The Assassin's Tale

name: Shifting Sands
rules_text:
Then graceful Auberol did cry
And fall to his knees
in the silent Sand
And mourned his
fair companions three
Buried alive in a lifeless Land
-- The Fae Quadrille of Wyr

name: Assassin
rules_text: "I crawled from my sepulcher and fell before my lord Khobai. 'Lienna', he said in his hollow whisper. 'Yellow witch of the Seelie. Find her and send her to the Silent Fathers.'" -- The Assassin's Tale

name: Raven Shroud
rules_text: "Thr'harruk is the eighth category of existence: Having been mortal, died, and been improperly buried, but nonetheless having realized Mystery, merged with the Darkness, and been utterly forgotten." -- The Keeper Code

name: Dire Portent
rules_text: "The Portent appears to a soldier in a nightmare. It is the holiest thing that can happen to him, a great honor. He awaits battle in grim terror, eagerly. But an Elf's touch will banish the Portent; the Elf will die, but the soldier will be robbed of a holy death." -- Diary of Sharul-Nar

name: Phantom
rules_text: Nomadic, Mountainwalk. Gets +3 missile damage against Visions. Phantom's attack does magical damage. (Magical damage ignores armor, immunities, and spells.)

name: Makva
rules_text: Nomadic, Flight.

name: Cruel Whim
rules_text: "Perhaps the Pretender wished to test his power, or perhaps he was ruled by his Shadow counselors. Strange orders issued from the Deep Vault of Kings, and mighty legions were told to die with dishonor." -- The Kandra Lineage Scrolls

name: Nihil's Minion
rules_text: "Gabromil's face was gray and slack. All around him his comrades died, but he made no move to save them. I saw him stiffen, and then his whole body changed " -- Account of an Elven archer, Battle of Thrgal

name: Shadowmasque
rules_text: Swordsman gets -N hand damage for having hit a Shadowmasque in combat, where N is the number of turns remaining.

name: Penumbra
rules_text: "All through the Shadowlands, constant horrible whispers kept our Fifth Battalion from sleep or meditation. Remarkable physical changes were observed " -- From a Djinni committee's report

name: Despond
rules_text: "Do not forgive us; our sin is too great. We, who claimed to best serve Hope, allowed the slaughter at Vask. We, who claimed to love purely, cannot repent or forgive. We bear our guilt down with us, into cowards' graves." -- The Last  Words of The Cheruli Mages

name: Griffin
rules_text: Nomadic, Flight. Cannot be targeted by Despair individual spells. Receives -1 damage from minions without Flight (before checking armor). Deals magical damage against Shadows. (Magical damage ignores armor, immunities, and spells.)

name: Centaurs
rules_text: Nomadic. Two attacks.

name: Inspiration
rules_text: "The portents for Clarity: All secrets will be made known, and all gates opened. Pure hearts schooled in trust will hold the Ring of Six Stones in trembling fingers." -- The Faldathi Prophecy

name: Leap of Faith
rules_text: "In the end, love can have no reason and no proof. Freedom lies in a commitment springing out of nothing. Giving up all attachment and identity is the fundamental generosity; and what remains is bliss." -- Ch'tai Academy discourse on Hope

name: Unerring Focus
rules_text: "Rage, spite, despair my constant temptations. Every moment a trial, a lesson, and a gift. Keep the Light always before me!" -- Heartsong, Imp disciple of Hope

name: Atonement
rules_text: The Imp Bloodspike was Zana's torturer at Bar-Thadarak. Later he embraced Hope and took the name Heartsong. On hearing this, Zana wept with gladness, and said: "Remorse is the gateway to joy."

name: Piercing Rays
rules_text: "As the arrows landed, my grim employers uttered unearthly wails. The bright faces of our ambushers were calm, compassionate. As an arrow grazed my arm, I thought of my own sins." -- Jan Palczred, mercenary

name: Tindelhunden
rules_text: Flight, Moves 2. Tindelhunden hunt their target group, switching sides if necessary. When target group dies, or is dispelled, Tindelhunden are removed from the game. (This does not trigger any death effects.) Humans get -1 damage per Tindelhund.

name: Alabaster Guardian
rules_text: Moves 2. Alabaster Guardian is player-controlled when it is in the 3x3 diamond centered on its target group. Otherwise, it seeks its target group. When its target group dies, or is dispelled, Alabaster Guardian becomes player-controlled.

name: Justicars
rules_text: "'Ateros!' I cried. But he spoke, and dashed my hopes of clemency: 'Ateros is no more. He has taken the Mask, and now there is only the Justicar.'" -- Diary of Vlad Hrothgar

name: Talisman of Souls
rules_text: "Only in being judged is the soul free. When Justice is complete, every penance paid, every wrong righted, the world in balance again, then the soul slips into the peaceful night to rest." -- From the "Initiatio Justicatorum"

name: Obeisance
rules_text: "Never seek power. Seek justice. Justice may call you to obey, or to command. That Justice may speak through you, abandon now your name, your memory and your relations " -- From the "Initiatio Justicatorum"

name: Nemesis
rules_text: At Cheol, Ngozi's son Ozande turned traitor and took the Justicar mask. It is said that while he lives, Ngozi will not return to reunite the tribes.

name: Chamberlain
rules_text: "Most of Ngozi's troops have been in Gargoyle servitude, and the sight of the stony creatures inspires them to violent rage; but at the memory of the Star Chamber, their courage fails " -- Kyman Firth, "The Siege of Groven's Weald"

name: Arboriads
rules_text: Before combat, Arboriads appear in forest adjacent to a non-friendly recruit group. After all combat, they disappear again. They get +4 hand damage against Death minions. Dispelled when killed.

name: Questing Beast
rules_text: Group becomes computer- controlled, cannot join groups or enter structures, and hunts its Questing Beast. If group fights its Questing Beast and causes it to leave play, every member of group gets +1 level. (Any recruits who join this group later are not affected.) Only one on a group.

name: Seelie Champion
rules_text:
His eyes were clear and golden
His voice rang like a bell
And at his throat a flower
Of our Lady Adriel
-- The Fae Quadrille of Wyr

name: Golden Grove
rules_text: From a Keeper legionnaire's report: "Then the sun rose, and the horror began. Everywhere awful white light, the nauseating scents of flowers, the unending noise of rushing water and a thousand flying and scurrying vermin enervated and confused us."

name: Filigreed Armor
rules_text: "While we have been able to create replicas, they do not have the properties of the original. This lends some credence to that upstart Fletcher's claim that the armor is grown by the Elves, not made " -- Forgemaster Ulrik's report to Odar

name: Adriel's Glamour
rules_text: "We were sore afraid at this intelligence, and raged. We cried: 'How could even foul Khobai plan to slay an entire land!' But our Lady smiled sweetly, and said: 'What he cannot Find, he cannot Slay.'" -- Theriel of Caenedor

name: Yeoman
rules_text: When archer hits a Keeper, the Keeper is killed. If the Keeper remains dead, Arhwazna is dispelled.

name: Gnome
rules_text: Nomadic, Mountainwalk. When Gnome moves, its old square becomes barren land, and its new square becomes mountain. Against Misfits, Gnome casts Mountain under opposing group at start of combat, and withdraws at the start of combat round 3. ("Moves" effects happen before any "enters square" effects.)

name: Combustioneers
rules_text: Nomadic. When a Combustioneer is killed, it explodes, inflicting 2 hit points of damage on its attacker.

name: Lost Mine
rules_text: "This war has ravaged the land, and our war machines have created horrors. We will accept the dictum of the Gargoyles: we will abandon these lands, and seek the mountains." -- Beobogh, Dwarven king

name: Panopticon
rules_text: Many youths would rather bear the shame of remaining guildless than apply to the Mirrorers' Guild -- politically powerless, religiously suspect, and the only guild ever to accept non-Dwarven applicants. The Panopticon of Master Mirrorer Fuko is the Guild's one triumph.

name: Deflection
rules_text: On the Eastern Continent, old wounds between the Dwarves and Djinn have healed, and collaboration thrives between the Kumatru Academy and the Guilds. Singh-Mukkajin's "Theory of the Properties of Fuligin" helped the Dwarven Tylemi clan develop force-deflection alloys.

name: Belvario's Horn
rules_text: "Sound the horn! Flush out the beasts, and let us close!" -- Guild Master Belvario on the hunt

name: Beobogh's Helm
rules_text: After the Great Wasting killed the Dwarven women, Beobogh the Armorer became King, and led the Dwarves into the mountains.

name: Itrokos Gate
rules_text: Shezif Xan, founder of the Itrokos Academy, invented the Xan topomorph function. It gives, for any two points, a topology under which they are adjacent, and has several interesting practical applications

name: Rakshasa
rules_text: Nomadic. Rakshasa is a computer- controlled archer/swordsman. Can be targeted by archer spells and swordsman spells. Cannot join groups, add recruits, or enter structures. Cannot be targeted by group spells.

name: Djinni Debate
rules_text: In the original 'Djinni debate', the Ch'tai and Sar'ul Academies split over the treatment of the Cyclopes. Rival polemicists stood and spoke nonstop for twelve years. Many nations now use the phrase to mean 'paralyzing indecision'.

name: Xin Shian Alcoves
rules_text: Xin Shia is the oldest of the Academies, and its teachings are nonsensical to many. But Xin Shian monks have meditated in their Alcoves since the childhood of the nations, and some say these meditations create all magic, or even that the whole world is but the monks' dream.

name: Tan'u'zhadhi Ploy
rules_text: The Tan'u'zhadhi created the Two Mages Game, now a wildly popular Djinn pastime. But none of the other Academies will play with the Tan'u'zhadhi, and not only because they cheat. They insist on using a Gorbak as a discard pile -- and legs have been lost to overeager Gorbaks.

name: Memorize
rules_text: "As the fires exploded around us, I cursed the old Djinn and begged him to let us all flee. But he sat watching the destruction with an expression of intense concentration " -- The Journey of Tansek

name: Sar'uli Overseer
rules_text: "Morality, compassion, hesitation, and fear are attachments that we must destroy. In the Mindless Ones, we can create and study extremes of experience that would kill a Djinn. Through subjugating them, we will free ourselves " -- Sar'uli manifesto

name: Jade Dragon
rules_text: Waterwalk. Two attacks. At start of combat against Imps, kills one random opposing Imp. At start of turn, is removed from the game (which does not trigger any death effects).

name: Goldcaps
rules_text: Territorial. Neutral. Goldcaps attack all groups, even if friendly.

name: Revelers
rules_text: Every revel has its price.

name: Scrying Pool
rules_text: "These blue ones hurt us, those talk big promises, and we stay patient. I say, enough waiting! Let us go to the Pool. There no creature can hide from me. There I can sort them out!" -- Tyu, Cyclops shaman, at a war council

name: Seed on the Wind
rules_text:
The dancing ripple
from a pebble's strike
The seed alighting in a patch of sand
Begins a wave
that bursts a city's dike
Begins a pine
that seeds a desert land
-- Song of the Satyrs

name: Tornado
rules_text: "Winds roared, the ground exploded, the trees fell into the sky! These Satyrs really know how to entertain a guest!" -- Elevava Glasshead, Misfit traveler

name: Protective Cover
rules_text: "Wild wood! These changed ones are no longer your children! In the fight tomorrow, O branches, O thorns -- trip and tangle, strike and strangle!" -- Diomesia in her cups

name: Cockatrice
rules_text: Minion gets -1 damage and +1 armor, cumulative, for having been hit by a Cockatrice. A minion who gets three or more Cockatrice's Gaze is killed.

name: Gnax
rules_text: Territorial. When a Gnax damages a Dwarf, the Dwarf gets a random individual spell (Chaos Feature). (A recruit who gets 3 or more Chaos Features changes into a Misfit.)

name: Bedlam
rules_text: "In the heart of Making lies the seed of Unmaking. After the horrific Dwarven war machines created the first Voids and Misfits, Chaos began to spread on its own." -- Az-arif, The Dominance Wars

name: Treacherous Armor
rules_text: "Tharn stood point, arrows glancing off his breastplate -- a well-made thing, that. Suddenly, it moved like a live thing: its plates puckered like mouths to guide the arrows into his vitals!" -- Dwarven eyewitness, Battle of Irrenburg

name: Entropia
rules_text: "The Shadows' magic had caught me in a trap, and each day I cursed the icy waves that lapped at the bleak city's edge. Then one morning the bitter seas were gone -- in their place stood a hooting, gibbering army of the strangest creatures I had ever seen " -- The Journey of Tansek

name: Erisian Blade
rules_text: "If one loves two, and two love three, how merry will those many be! If three love four, and four love five, how mighty will the many thrive!" -- The Carpist, Misfit bard

name: Thermites
rules_text: Minion gets -1 armor, cumulative, for having been hit by Thermites.

name: Hellion
rules_text: Nomadic. After all combat, if Hellion fought only non-Justice minions this turn, she changes sides.

name: Wyrmling
rules_text: Recruit gets +2 armor for having helped its group to kill a Wyrmling.

name: Weapon Master
rules_text: "Other Human bands have hunters, traders, herders, and mothers, but there are only combatants among the Farouki. They grow in numbers by adopting the orphans of war, and bear arms from age six " -- Alicar, History of the Human Bands of Acheron

name: Encampment
rules_text: Structure cannot be targeted by spells. When structure creates any novices, those novices are eliminated. At start of turn of casting +3 (or earliest turn thereafter), if not occupied, Colony is dispelled (which destroys colony), group reappears, and is fully healed. If captured, target group is removed from the game. (This does not trigger any death effects.)

name: Armistice
rules_text: "Peace is an excellent ploy." -- Ngozi, the Book of War

name: Bloodlust
rules_text:
His eyeballs bulged
And filled with blood
His hair stood straight
And as he spun in rage
His scream tore every heart in twain
-- Epic poem, Vrathi tribe

name: Iron Shillelagh
rules_text: "So my dear Ogi has created something wonderful, eh? Well, we'll just see about that. Bwahahahaha!" -- Fingle, Misfit hero and Dwarven heretic

name: Savannah
rules_text: The tribe can only prosper and grow as long as there are animals to hunt and plants to gather. When the land is crowded, a powerful shaman can spread the savannah outwards, like the unfolding of a cloth.

name: Organ Grinder
rules_text: "Always we were! Always! Fire and pain! But Bitterus brought us out here. So many pretty things out here to crush and hurt! Thank Bitterus - tear his horns off! Play a happy song!" -- Spittle Toothcrack, Imp historian and lyricist

name: Destiny Manifest
rules_text: "Freedom is an illusion. The true path is set. There is only the freedom to stray from it, and that is no freedom at all. How much better, then, when that choice is removed." -- From the "Initiatio Justicatorum"

name: Gatemouth
rules_text: Nomadic. Neutral. At start of turn, gets -1 maximum hit point. Before combat, all adjacent groups are teleported to random unoccupied non-structure squares not adjacent to this Gatemouth.

name: Seelie Court
rules_text: When the Keeper Legions advanced upon the Court, Lord Auberol asked the Fae to hide it. They hid it well, and to this day the Court is difficult to visit.

name: Ice Age
rules_text: "The portents for Strife: Water will embrace no more, nor quench raging thirst " -- The Faldathi Prophecy

name: Zurzavar
rules_text: "I can't say I know if we ever reached the town -- every time I looked at it, it shifted; a wall one moment was a cave the next, a passageway would turn out to be solid stone -- we found ourselves looking back at the town before we'd really entered it." -- Diary of Vlad Hrothgar

name: Doppelganger
rules_text: Nomadic. Two attacks. At start of combat against monsters, Doppelganger kills the first opposing monster, copies its hand and missile damage, attacks per round, armor, hit points, level, and house, and is fully healed.

name: Firewalking
rules_text: "That the Ch'tai offer no credible theory to explain this could be mere failure. That they refuse to try, insisting that pure Faith is necessary, is treason. They are seduced by Hope, and should be stripped of their status " -- Sar'ul Academy polemic

name: Lava Flow
rules_text: It is said the Pyromancers of the Seulik tribe know how to enrage the mountains themselves.

name: Fuligin
rules_text: All other Dwarven Guilds now accept apprentices from any clan and debate their methods openly with other Masters; but the Metallurgists of the Tylemi clan keep their secrets. Not even the Armorers, who receive their ingots of Fuligin, know what it is.

name: Syzygy
rules_text:
There is a moment, then, in every life
And wild lives teem
in the abundant green
When chance alignments
of the great and small
Reveal the Pattern seldom seen
-- Song of the Satyrs

name: Cessao Spiral
rules_text: "Such was the beauty of Narya's song that many creatures followed her into the dream. Some like Grandmother Walkinglizard, dream still, and that is why you do not see them anymore upon the land." -- The Collected Folklore of the Monocular Aboriginals

name: Oblivion
rules_text: "Our ears were full of ashes, and I heard no sounds of dying until the dark point of a spear found my arm. I awoke to find that all was lost, and that it bothered me not at all." -- Vahrus

name: Pyre
rules_text: "Cast aside the firefly magics of these glittering monsters! And if the Silent Fathers will it, may this night find us Tasharaf!" -- Khobai to the Jackals before the Battle of Groven's Weald

name: Shield of Vela
rules_text: Minion gets -1 damage and +1 armor, cumulative, for having been hit by Gorgon, Gorgon's Head, or Shield of Vela. A minion who gets three or more Gorgon's Gaze is killed.

name: Sword of Zana II
rules_text: Zana called her generals and blessed their swords. "You are my most faithful," she said, "and you will need your faith today, for it is a terrible power I give you."

name: Apocalypse II
rules_text: Some cheat Death once. Yet in the end, Death always collects.

name: Bleak Isle II
rules_text: Many desperate souls are drawn to the crags of the Bleak Isle. Some choose to remain here, cut off from the outside world. They are called Programmers.

name: Neb's Mirage
rules_text: There is water just beyond the next hill. It's only a little further away.

name: Beta Mana
rules_text: Check Mantis at beta.sanctum.nioga.net. Test gray 'feedback' entries. Send text reports to beta@sanctum.nioga.net, as a simple list of 'Confirm' or 'Deny' lines. Send bug logs (zipped) to bugs@nioga.net.

name: Elven Piper
rules_text: "Adriel's honor guard are beautifully attired, but that sound they make can be heard in distant Volgaria " -- Ogi, Kingmaker

name: Cauldron of Strife
rules_text: "Strife burns, burns. Dip right hand in the cauldron, feel the flesh dissolve. Left hand in the cauldron, two hands at war, power for the taking!" -- Impish recipe

name: Rite of Mystery
rules_text: "'I know not how those Mystery-mongers gain power,' said the old Satyr, slamming down his flagon. 'And I don't care to know.'" -- Journal of Lienna: Volume IX

name: Oculus of Will
rules_text: This metal device is so well made that it magnifies the will of Guildsman and outsider alike.

name: Wolf Pack
rules_text: Territorial.

name: Sign of Order
rules_text:
In all of Nature's promulgation
some design
Faint, inscrutable, yet
sure discerned
Penetrates the whole, drives fuse
through flower
Requires the robin's taking
of the worm
-- Song of the Satyrs

name: Source of World
rules_text: How can the Leechwood grow without Forest or Treeman? The world supplies the material, Strife supplies the tools

name: Point of Clarity
rules_text: "Study tactics, and know your enemy -- this is the way to victory." -- Ngozi, The Book of War

name: Allies
rules_text: "The pounding of the battering rams stopped, and then we heard the trills of trumpets. And we knew that our friends had arrived." -- Joden Flightweaver on the Siege at Trokain

name: Abatement
rules_text: "It is a simple matter to strip all your nonsense away, and return the land to her folk!" -- Acornbelly, boasting to Fingle

name: Fear
rules_text: Once a Shadow has paralyzed his victim with fear, he can plunder his psyche and blast his every hope to ashes.

name: Fleetness
rules_text: Cyclopes distinguish between Xa Macao ("world walking") and Xa Cessao ("mystery walking"), a spiritual plane also called "World of Mystery," "other side" and "ancestor's home".

name: Complacency
rules_text: "Mother Mud is warm, Sister Sky is tender; shut your eye, little one, shut your eye and sleep " -- Cyclops lullaby

name: Circle of Wisdom
rules_text: If a tribe is afflicted by a Mala Ozo, they form a loose circle, drumming and chanting to drive away the curse and purify the group.

name: Slay Monster
rules_text: There is an ancient, bitter theological debate in the Keeper priesthood about whether all creatures can realize Mystery, and thus may be embalmed.

name: Bolstering Spirit
rules_text: "If they slay us, we may at last embrace the Night we long for: but if they fell the pillars of our city, who will bury us?" -- Sharul-Nar

name: Winnowing Sigil
rules_text: Keepers believe ideas, ambitions and identities are repulsive but inevitable burdens in life. The prized Sigil scours these away.

name: Power
rules_text: Kumatru Academy, 2nd year subjects: Focus, Domination and Dominion, Law, Poetics, Transcendence of Physicality, Emanative Statics.

name: Hypnotic Trance
rules_text: "When the decadent Tan'u'zhadhi exhaust themselves controlling all their pets, they can barely manage to confuse them!" --Turgan Qef, Kumatru Academy

name: Venom'd Blade
rules_text: "Oro had been grazed by the strange blade. He fought on, but his great frame shook, and his features were twisted into a smile. Cyclopes never smile." -- Diary of Vlad Hrothgar

name: Second Chance
rules_text: "The Gargoyle breathed deep, the stones shook and the bones knit. In the darkness, he seemed almost kind." -- Kyman Firth, "The Siege of Groven's Weald"

name: Homunculi
rules_text: Nomadic. Homunculi get +1 missile damage in combat round 1 only.

name: Warlord
rules_text: Warlord is a swordsman. Warlord gets +3 hand damage. Other swordsmen in Warlord's group get +1 hand damage.

name: Forbidden Ichor
rules_text: A vile potion favored by Misfits traveling in the world for its powers to disguise tendril, claw and mandible.

name: Unholy Aura
rules_text: "The Imp's stroke was well timed, but his sword shattered against my body. 'Ah, foolish little apprentice', I said quietly. 'Now, my sport begins.'" -- the Infernalist Javhakr

name: Faerie Knight
rules_text: "Once in a great while the Fae choose a champion amongst us, to tell their stories and teach us of the ancient ages." -- Alicar, Elven bard

name: Chaos Feature
rules_text: "These external changes, however grotesque, are only symptomatic of more extreme internal realignments and dissolutions." -- Panoman Jar, Vivisectionist Second Class

name: Dracha's Trade
rules_text: Dracha is cruel but fair.

name: Spectre's Curse
rules_text: The victim first struggles when the Shadow falls over her. Then, when she realizes the bleeding will not stop, she succumbs to icy despair.

name: Pacifism
rules_text: "To achieve the greatest respect for life is to give up any destruction of it." -- Lienna

name: Carbuncle
rules_text: "Blood boiling in rage" is not merely a figure of speech among Imps.

name: Devouring
rules_text: The foulest Imp recipes describe not how the meat is prepared, but the victim's state of mind as he is consumed -- which requires a meal both sentient and conscious. 'Incensed, unfettered' is a most potent brew, in which energy is released and reharnessed, 'from one belly to another'. -- Al-Talataa Umunayyil, Tan'u'zhadhi Academy

name: Dissention
rules_text: It is a pastime among Imps to give counsel that strengthens the client's resolve, deepens emotional attachments, rebuts by insult rather than insight, and advocates dominance over compromise. Supplicants are pleased to hear such flattering words, and the outcome amuses the Imp.

name: Eat And Run
rules_text: "Toebreak devised this strategem, to replenish expenditures of energy by redistributing, ha, weight! And the first short stick he drew himself. Huzzah!" -- Gash Tic, Imp troubadour

name: Fiend
rules_text: Nomadic, Flight, Moves 2.

name: Implosion
rules_text: Imps are quite willing to die so long as they do not die alone. This makes them exceptionally dangerous when cornered. -- Ngozi, The Book of War

name: Salted Wounds
rules_text: "Imps' bodies secrete certain salts and noxious compounds that interfere with healing. They must be kept far away from open wounds." -- Journal of Lienna: Volume VII

name: Scars of Kolekh
rules_text:
Tuition -- Arm and leg
Benefit -- No crowd too tough
-- Kolekh's Body Art

name: Soul Eater
rules_text: 'Ask me not how quickly I'll sip,' whispered the voice from the other side of the portal. 'Ask yourself how much you can thresh.' -- Padrek-Dar, Observations on the Greater Rituals of Strife

name: Winged Monkeys
rules_text: Nomadic, Flight. Withdraws at the start of combat round 3. At end of combat, if both groups are still alive, enemy group is carried to a random adjacent unoccupied non-structure square, and Winged Monkeys are removed from the game. (This does not trigger any death effects.)

name: Big Rock
rules_text: Cyclops children at play throw enormous rocks, competing by weight and distance. As they mature, they learn to impart spin, and throw for accuracy. Many hunters carry carefully polished stones larger than two fists, or knee-high pieces of heavy wood, shaped into wings.

name: Brother Wolf
rules_text: Brother Wolf is a swordsman. Other members of his group no longer attack. When any other member of his group is hit (after checking armor), Wolf receives an equal amount of magical damage, and the member who was hit receives no damage. When Wolf dies, his group withdraws, ending combat. Only one in a group.

name: Brown Striders
rules_text: Minion gets -1 hand damage, cumulative, for having been bitten by a Brown Strider.

name: Lion Spirit
rules_text: The lion is native to the grasslands of the Eastern Continent, where it preys on migrating herds of large game. Nomads call it Voda Lea, Grandfather Hunting-Tribe, and those who claim it as their totem fear no lesser beast. -- Uyden's Handbook

name: Vision Quest
rules_text: Recruit gets +2 levels because it completed a Vision Quest.

name: Visionary
rules_text: He is Xa Cessao, walking in mystery. He leaves his Ge, his sight, in the Cessao. He is Xa Macao, walking in the world. He is Ge Cessao, seeing in mystery. No enemy hides from his Ge, no weakness turns from his Ge, no distance dulls his Ge. -- Cyclops oral tradition

name: Bone Chariot
rules_text: The heat of this dry land may kill the flesh once. Fortunate, then, that our mounts no longer feel it. -- Diary of Sharul-Nar

name: Day of the Dead
rules_text: To arms! To arms! Your duty is not done this day!

name: Drought
rules_text: At start of turn, square's terrain changes sequentially, from water to swamp to plains to barren land to desert. (Dispelled at end of sequence, or if terrain changes from this sequence. Has no effect on squares with other terrain types.)

name: Ghost Town
rules_text: "Mere miles behind us lay the desert. Yet the town ahead spoke of true desolation. We entered near dusk, but were met only with hollow wind and broken homes. No evidence of inhabitants could be found - no foodstuffs, no clothing, no graves. It was as if there had never been anyone there at all." -- Journal of Lienna: Volume XI

name: Harmattan
rules_text: This hot, dry wind blows from the desert to the sea for days at a time, and gathers such dust that the sun is obscured in haze. Keeper legions walk in time with the swirling sand, so that distant observers cannot tell which is which.

name: Sacrifice
rules_text: Thr'zzan is the eleventh category of existence: Being mortal and dying, by starvation, suffocation, or loss of fluids, due to having been buried, embalmed, or impaled, with ceremonial honors, particularly if unwilling. Keepers may earn this privilege through noble rank or great deeds, while the rich simply buy it; the poor can only hope to win it in a lottery.

name: Sandstorm
rules_text: Keepers are widely rumored to control the sands themselves, which they allow, as it strengthens their position. The truth is simpler: They minimize risk through diligent study of the seasons and the winds; and when this fails them, they go quietly, which others mistake for a lack of casualties.

name: Soul Jar
rules_text: In the moment that a conscious soul draws back the curtain between worlds and plunges into the dark abyss, its body, crude broken ship of its voyage above, may be physically thrust after it into the Lower Realms. This interloper is soon sent back, its owner forced to pilot it anew; it reawakens with the keenest disappointment, and a hollow chuckle in its ears.

name: Reaper
rules_text: The Gleaning is a sacred task in which Keepers scour a battlefield and grant proper burials to all who fell, for persons of all nations, if so tended, may yet meet the Silent Fathers. Those fallen who might have survived are most revered, and are buried alive with ceremonial honors -- and such encouragement as is necessary.

name: The Withering
rules_text: Recent advances in plagues of mass dispersal have led to a popular shift in interpreting the ancient Keeper Code. A silent majority now believe The Withering fulfills a key requirement for Thr'zzan, that the body fails while the mind remains whole, so that its victims can still realize Mystery.

name: Blight
rules_text: Even the land itself can know Despair. When the Shadows pour over the terrain, they leave nothing but sorrow in their wake.

name: Frost Dragon
rules_text: Territorial, Flight. At start of combat, casts Frostbite (-3 maximum hit points, +1 maximum hit points per turn for 3 turns) on opposing group. When Frost Dragon damages an opponent, opponent does not attack in the next combat round.

name: Immateriality
rules_text: To Shadows, the obstacles of the physical world are insignificant. They penetrate stone and wood as easily as their cursed whispers pierce your skull.

name: Shadow Friend
rules_text: The Shadow is expert in finding the fond memories, self-indulgent whimsies, little boasts and longings that accrue to the brains of mortals in this imperfect, restless, too-bright life, and crushing them to ash and silence  -- The Dead Letter of the Keeper Zaduth-Jar

name: Emancipation
rules_text: Monster group becomes neutral (but keeps previous control by computer or player). (Neutral groups move last, attack all other groups, and have "half-initiative" in combat.)

name: Empathy
rules_text: Followers of Hope feel the pain of others.

name: Hand of Zana
rules_text: Hope can move you, sometimes in a literal sense.

name: Illender
rules_text: Where the ground is poisonous, let it be simple. Where dust lies, let grasses grow. Where marshes reign, let lush forest live.

name: Deva's Quiver
rules_text: The Quivers Deva made were things of beauty and awe, filled with the essence of Hope itself. Arrows steeped in their light pierce any darkness of soul and strip away all doubt.

name: Vale of Peace
rules_text: Square cannot be targeted by summoning spells. Monsters cannot enter square.

name: Volunteers
rules_text: Those who flock to the cause, Hope calls volunteers. But the Shadows claim they are forced and have no free will.

name: Zana's Champion
rules_text: Zana's Champion is a swordsman. Zana's Champion counts as a structure for Initiative purposes. Champion's group and its members cannot be targeted by any group spells or individual spells. Only one in play at a time.

name: Exile
rules_text: Monster group leaves player control. If mobile, group moves toward the edge of board. At start of turn, if group is in an edge square, group is removed from the game. (This does not trigger any death effects.)

name: Gilt
rules_text: Gifts from above can be both blessings and curses. At times one must be protected from both and let truth be the guide. -- Sayings of Pantogar

name: Hapax Legomenon
rules_text: It is always the best policy to speak the truth, for in these times you may do so only once.

name: Injunction
rules_text: As the enemy draws blissfully from the Well, so do we dam the waters from which it is fed.

name: Jade Gargoyle
rules_text: Jade Gargoyle is a swordsman. Jade Gargoyle cannot be targeted by individual spells. When any individual spell is cast on a member of group, and does not cause member to leave the group, a copy of that spell is cast on Jade Gargoyle (as if cast by Jade Gargoyle's owner).

name: Dracha's Decree
rules_text: The world of trade is reminiscent of a wonderland: everything seemingly is, yet apparently isn't, simultaneously. It is a world in which the law is so vague that there is no way of knowing if its a good deal, or a good death.

name: Law of the Land
rules_text: Square cannot be targeted by any spells. Duration: Through turn of casting +5.

name: Prohibition
rules_text: "Truly variety is the spice of life, but those damn Gargoyles can have such a one-track mind sometimes." -- Dinous, Satyr wine merchant

name: Adriel's Mantle
rules_text: Adriel ventured into the world with her followers, but was not rash enough to be a target.

name: Auberol's Grace
rules_text: Recruit gets +4 maximum and current hit points because it was created in a structure with Auberol's Grace.

name: Cadre
rules_text: "Fire at Will!"

name: Clandestine
rules_text: Recruit is concealed because it was created in a structure with Clandestine. (Concealed minions cannot be targeted by enemy individual spells.)

name: Elixir Vitae
rules_text: Recruit gets +6 maximum hit points.

name: Fae Gift
rules_text: When the Fae leave the Court, they remain true to their beliefs and inflict healing on others.

name: Fae Quadrille
rules_text: "The dance gives to the joy of life, but the grounds we dance upon are hallowed for that day." -- MiKayla Serena, daughter of Auberol

name: Lienna's Steed
rules_text: "Astride my steed, I had little to fear. Though I will pass from the forest, my steed will live on as long as the forests remain, and will help when needed." -- Journal of Lienna: Volume XIII

name: Seelie Favor
rules_text: May the shiny hag from the hated Court be tormented forever! Her malignant bewitchment has left us open to the ravages of the desert. Our honor in the face of the Silent Fathers has been lost!

name: Vivant
